Title: Changes in the Proanthocyanidin Composition and Related Gene Expression in Bilberry (Vaccinium myrtillus L.) Tissues
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and defense-related polyphenols; contribute to nutritional value and deterrence of early feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols, producing epicatechin-type and epigallocatechin-type flavan-3-ols for PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate monomeric precursors for proanthocyanidin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in stem and rhizome; also relatively high in leaves; decreases during fruit development toward mature fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in stem and rhizome; also relatively high in leaves; decreases during fruit development toward mature fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling across tissues and fruit developmental stages; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-DAD-ESI-3Q-MS; correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit, leaf, stem, rhizome; soluble and insoluble PAs accumulate strongly in stem, rhizome, and developing fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with accumulation of epicatechin/epigallocatechin and PA biosynthesis; high ANR expression corresponds to strong bias toward cis-flavan-3-ols and higher PA-related flux in stem and rhizome.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmANR is the only ANR gene identified/reported for bilberry in this study context. 2. Its expression is highest in stem and rhizome and declines during fruit maturation. 3. VmANR expression correlates with epicatechin and epigallocatechin levels, supporting a role in supplying cis-flavan-3-ol precursors for PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-DAD-ESI-3Q-MS and UHPLC-DAD-ESI-Orbitrap-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer that the single bilberry ANR likely accepts both PC- and PD-related precursors for epicatechin-type biosynthesis; all tissues showed a strong 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ol bias indicating high ANR activity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and defense-related polyphenols; contribute to nutritional value and deterrence of early feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Leucoanthocyanidin reductase; conserved RFLP, ICCN, THD motifs and NADP-binding GXXGXXG motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to 2,3-trans-flavan-3-ols that contribute monomer units for PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: leucoanthocyanidins, likely leucodelphinidin-preferred as inferred by authors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate monomeric precursors for proanthocyanidin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in flowers, stem, and rhizome; decreases toward large unripe fruit then increases again toward ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in flowers, stem, and rhizome; decreases toward large unripe fruit then increases again toward ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cloning and sequencing; conserved motif and phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern links VmLAR1a to PA/flavan-3-ol accumulation from different flavonoid branches, with authors suggesting preference toward leucodelphinidin-derived products.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmLAR1a was isolated as one of three bilberry LAR genes and contains conserved LAR motifs. 2. Its transcript is high in flowers, stem, and rhizome and rises again during ripening after an earlier decline. 3. The expression pattern suggests contribution to PA biosynthesis and possible preference toward leucodelphinidin-derived trans-flavan-3-ols.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Modifying LAR activity may alter PA polymerization and flux balance with other flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cloning/sequencing; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-DAD-ESI-3Q-MS and Orbitrap-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note that LARs may also participate in regulation of PA polymerization beyond trans-flavan-3-ol synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and defense-related polyphenols; contribute to nutritional value and deterrence of early feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Leucoanthocyanidin reductase; conserved RFLP, ICCN, THD motifs and NADP-binding GXXGXXG motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to 2,3-trans-flavan-3-ols contributing monomers for PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: leucoanthocyanidins, likely leucocyanidin-preferred as inferred by authors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate monomeric precursors for proanthocyanidin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in flowers, stem, and rhizome; increases toward large unripe fruit then decreases during ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in flowers, stem, and rhizome; increases toward large unripe fruit then decreases during ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cloning and sequencing; conserved motif and phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern links VmLAR1b to PA/flavan-3-ol accumulation, especially early fruit stages and likely procyanidin-related trans-flavan-3-ol production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmLAR1b is one of three isolated bilberry LAR genes with conserved LAR signatures. 2. Its expression peaks around the large unripe fruit stage and is also high in stem and rhizome. 3. The expression profile suggests a role in catechin/procyanidin-associated PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cloning/sequencing; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-DAD-ESI-3Q-MS and Orbitrap-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer VmLAR1b may prefer leucocyanidin as substrate based on expression relative to metabolite patterns.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and defense-related polyphenols; contribute to nutritional value and deterrence of early feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Leucoanthocyanidin reductase; conserved RFLP, ICCN, THD motifs and NADP-binding GXXGXXG motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to 2,3-trans-flavan-3-ols contributing monomers for PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: leucoanthocyanidins, likely leucocyanidin-preferred as inferred by authors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate monomeric precursors for proanthocyanidin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in stem and rhizome; decreases during fruit development toward ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in stem and rhizome; decreases during fruit development toward ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cloning and sequencing; conserved motif and phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern links VmLAR2 to PA/flavan-3-ol accumulation, with stronger association to early developmental and nonfruit tissues and likely procyanidin-related trans-flavan-3-ol production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmLAR2 is a third bilberry LAR gene cloned in full length and verified by conserved motifs/phylogeny. 2. Its expression is high in stem and rhizome and decreases during fruit ripening. 3. The pattern suggests participation in catechin/procyanidin-associated PA biosynthesis in developing tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may influence PA polymerization and broader flavonoid flux allocation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene cloning/sequencing; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-DAD-ESI-3Q-MS and Orbitrap-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer VmLAR2, like VmLAR1b, may prefer leucocyanidin as substrate based on developmental expression pattern.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Increase antioxidant and anthelmintic activity and nutritional value through PD-rich PA composition.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B-ring intermediates to channel flux toward the delphinidin/prodelphinidin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: flavonoid intermediates of the B-ring hydroxylation step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3',5'-hydroxylated flavonoid intermediates leading to delphinidin-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch step supported by expression-metabolite association
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Exceptionally high in late fruit developmental stages S4 and S5; lower in other tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Exceptionally high in late fruit developmental stages S4 and S5; lower in other tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling across tissues and fruit stages; metabolite profiling of PD versus PC subunits
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing and ripe fruit, plus other bilberry tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher VmF3'5'H expression during ripening is associated with increased PD subunits and a shift from the cyanidin/procyanidin branch toward the delphinidin/prodelphinidin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmF3'5'H expression strongly increases in ripening and ripe berries. 2. This increase parallels accumulation of PD subunits and reduction in PC/PD ratio during fruit development. 3. The data indicate a biosynthetic shift toward the delphinidin branch in ripe fruit, enhancing PD-rich PA composition and nutritional value.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-DAD-ESI-3Q-MS and Orbitrap-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly links increased VmF3'5'H expression to rising PD subunits during ripening.
